subject: Sociology
title: Black Lives Matter
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Black Lives Matter.

hook.text: A hashtag became a movement after Trayvon Martin's killer walked free. By 2020, it was the largest U.S. protest ever.

buildup.text: Black Lives Matter began in 2013 as a decentralized network fighting police violence and structural racism.

discovery.text: Unlike 1960s movements, BLM has no single leader. It's organized through social media, local chapters, and coalitions.

twist.text: Critics called it divisive. Supporters argued that naming Black lives was necessary because the system devalued them.

climax.text: BLM shifted public opinion on policing and racism, even among people who had previously denied systemic inequality.

punchline.text: Three words forced a nation to confront what it refused to see.

quiz.question: How does BLM differ from the 1960s civil rights movement?
options: It is decentralized with no single leader
It has a strict hierarchical structure
It focuses exclusively on voting rights
